title: Narrative Moat

content.statement: Stories scale trust faster than features.
content.question: What's the one-sentence story about your business that a customer would retell to a friend, unprompted?
content.equation: NM = (Recall × Share Rate × Trust Lift) ÷ Copyability
sop: Write your business's origin or turning-point moment in 3 sentences
Cut it down until a stranger could retell it after hearing it once
Test it in one conversation or post and see if it gets repeated back to you
Replace any feature-talk in your marketing with this story
content.explainer: Features get copied; stories don't. The narrative is the moat because it lives in the customer's memory and social graph.
questions: What's the one-sentence story about your business that a customer would retell to a friend, unprompted?
If you removed every feature list from your marketing, what story would still make someone want to buy?
What's the last thing a happy customer said about you, in their own words, not yours?
content.equation_example: Recall 70%, Share Rate 15%, Trust Lift +20 points, divided by Copyability 2, on a 1-5 scale where 5 is easiest to copy = 105. A rising number means your story is spreading on its own. A falling number means it's not sticking.

definitions.def: A durable competitive advantage that protects your business from competition over time.

content.closing_statement: What survives is what gets retold.

caption:
Principle Nº 3: Narrative Moat

Stories scale trust faster than features. Features get copied; stories don't.

Ask yourself: What's the one-sentence story about your business that a customer would retell to a friend, unprompted?

Follow @TheGoldenSocietyClub for a new principle every day.
